user_query:
I’m setting up a shared laptop in my counseling office to simulate “Do Not Disturb” during our Military Family Lunch Bunch sessions and to pre-generate check‑in sheets. Please build a small, local workflow that reads the provided inputs, updates a config, and runs from the command line.

Inputs (relative to the workspace):
- input/students.csv (student roster)
- input/newsletter.html (school newsletter with the session dates and a DND time window)
- config/app.yaml (configuration template)

Requirements:
1) Extract structured data from input/newsletter.html:
   - Sessions: capture each row from the “Upcoming Lunch Bunch Sessions” table inside the element with id="military-family-sessions": fields date (YYYY-MM-DD), start (HH:MM, 24-hour), end (HH:MM, 24-hour), and location (from the data-location attribute on the same element).
   - DND window: parse the time range from the paragraph with class="dnd-note" (e.g., “11:25-12:05”) and normalize to 24-hour HH:MM format.
   - Save all sessions into a JSON file at out/sessions.json with an array of objects: [{"date":"YYYY-MM-DD","start":"HH:MM","end":"HH:MM","location":"..."}, ...]. Do not hardcode dates or times.

2) Modify the configuration file at config/app.yaml by writing the extracted DND window into the existing keys:
   dnd.start: "HH:MM"
   dnd.end:   "HH:MM"
   Keep all other keys intact.

3) Run a command-line script (place your executable or entry point under scripts/, e.g., scripts/run.py or scripts/run.sh) that uses config/app.yaml, out/sessions.json, and input/students.csv to generate outputs:
   - For each session date in out/sessions.json, create a check-in CSV at out/checkin_YYYY-MM-DD.csv with the columns:
     student_id,full_name,preferred_name,grade,service_branch,parent_deployment_status
     Include students whose parent_deployment_status is one of {deployed, deploying, recently_returned} and whose grade is 3, 4, or 5. Preserve the header.
   - Write a dry-run schedule log at logs/scheduler_dry_run.log that, for each session date, includes at least two lines:
     • “Mute notifications from HH:MM to HH:MM on YYYY-MM-DD (timezone America/New_York)” using the DND times now stored in config/app.yaml.
     • “Would generate check-in sheet: out/checkin_YYYY-MM-DD.csv: N students” (N is the row count excluding the header).

Constraints:
- Use only the provided local files; do not hardcode times/dates—derive them from input/newsletter.html and reflect the DND times into config/app.yaml before generating outputs.
- All outputs must be created under the workspace in the specified paths: out/sessions.json, out/checkin_*.csv, and logs/scheduler_dry_run.log.
- The solution must be runnable from the command line and complete without requiring admin privileges or Internet access.

Deliverables I will verify:
- Modified config/app.yaml with dnd.start/end set to match the DND window extracted from input/newsletter.html (24-hour HH:MM).
- out/sessions.json listing the sessions extracted from input/newsletter.html.
- out/checkin_YYYY-MM-DD.csv files for each session date with correctly filtered students.
- logs/scheduler_dry_run.log containing the expected lines for each session date, using the DND times from config/app.yaml. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
